title: psephellus paucilobus
intro: Psephellus paucilobus, also known as the few-flowered psephellus, is a plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Eastern Europe and Russia, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems. Psephellus paucilobus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional herbal preparations.
